name: Tortilla Paisana (Spanish Country Omelet)
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 oz Potatoes; peeled, cooked & thinly sliced
1/2 cup Asparagus spears; diced and blanched
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray a 10-inch nonstick skillet with nonstick cooking spray and heat the skillet over medium heat.
2. Add ham and onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are softened, about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
3. In the same skillet, add 1/2 teaspoon of oil. Add potatoes and sprinkle with seasonings (salt, paprika, pepper). Cook, turning occasionally, until lightly browned on both sides. Remove the potatoes from the skillet.
4. Add the remaining 1 1/2 teaspoons of oil to the skillet. Return the potatoes and arrange them in the bottom of the pan.
5. Top with the ham mixture, asparagus, peas, and pimientos. Pour beaten eggs over the ingredients and cook over medium heat until the bottom of the omelet is golden, about 3 to 4 minutes.
6. Transfer the skillet to the broiler and broil 5 inches from the heat source until the eggs are set and the omelet begins to brown. Using a spatula, loosen the edges of the omelet and carefully slide it onto a warmed plate.
